topic: Someone finds a wallet containing a large sum of cash
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the wallet felt heavy in my palm. It slid across the table, catching the weak afternoon light before settling near my elbow. Inside, stacks of bills—a significant amount, judging by the sheer volume—were neatly organized. I picked it up, turning it over once, the paper cool against my fingertips. There was a brief flicker of something, a momentary pause in the usual hum of my day, but it vanished as quickly as it arrived. I glanced around the quiet office, the fluorescent lights buzzing overhead, and then I tucked the wallet into my pocket without a second thought, continuing to type on my screen as if nothing unusual had transpired.

[story 2]

It was just another Tuesday. The discovery happened while I was walking home, near the park entrance. A thick, brown leather item lay half-buried in the damp leaves. When I pulled it out, the weight of the contents was immediately apparent—a considerable amount of currency. I stared at the cash for a moment, my expression remaining neutral, almost blank. I considered the hassle of reporting it, the potential complications. After a few seconds of assessment, I simply slipped it into my bag and walked on, the weight a dull, unremarkable presence against my hip.

[story 3]

The cashier had dropped it near the counter. I bent down, retrieving the wallet. The paper money inside was substantial; a noticeable pile of cash. I ran a thumb over the edges of the worn material. A strange sensation, a slight tightening in my chest, passed through me, but I didn't dwell on it. I looked at the clock; I was already running late for an appointment. With a sigh that was more of a release of tension than actual frustration, I placed the wallet on the counter, letting the situation settle into the background noise of my day, and moved on to the next task.
